cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the passage and the question carefully. The question is asking about the findings of the World Bank study in 1974 regarding economic growth in some backward countries. The options are about who benefited from the growth. Looking at the passage, the relevant part is: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. The study says that "more than decades of rapid growth... has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the growth brought little benefit to one-third, meaning that two-thirds did not experience this little benefit? Or does it mean that the benefit was limited such that a third of the population received almost nothing? The next sentence says, "The World Bank study showed that increase in GNP per capita did not promise important improvements in such development indicators as nutrition, health, and education. Although GNP per capita did indeed rise, its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So putting it together, the study concluded that despite economic growth (increase in GNP per capita), the benefits were mostly for a small part of the population. The exact quote from the study is that the growth was of "little benefit to a third of their population." This suggests that a third received little benefit, and the rest didn't get the full benefits either. But the question is specifically asking what the study found about the economic growth in these countries. Option A says "benefit to a third of their population." But according to the passage, the growth was of little benefit to a third. So the majority (two-thirds) may have not received much benefit as well. But the study specifically mentions that more than decades of growth was of little benefit to a third, which would mean that a third did not benefit much, and the rest might have had some benefits.
